Use Claude Code for FREE with N2 (New Agentic API) + Voice-Build Apps Live

The video demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free by plugging in the newly released N2 agentic “mixture of experts” API via an open-source project called Free Claude Code. N2 is based on Qwen 3.5 architecture, supports text and images, is designed for coding, tool use, deep research, and long-horizon agent workflows, offers a 262K context window, and is available on OpenRouter as a free API (with occasional token limits). The presenter shows voice-operating Free Claude Code to generate apps (e.g., a to-do list, habit tracker, meditation timer), preview results, and save every build in a workspace, plus adding skills like Hyperframes for video creation. They also show N2 working in Hermes Agent and explain integrating it into an “idea factory” pipeline and a shared memory dashboard, with setup available via the AI Profit Boardroom or the open-source project.

videos.title: 11.06.26 - RIFT Presentation
videos.summary_source_text:
11.06.26 - RIFT Presentation 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

RIFT is a next-generation trading interface built on top of Hyperliquid, designed to simplify and accelerate how users interact with modern financial markets.

Instead of manually buying and selling assets, RIFT allows you to take positions on price movements (up or down) across crypto and other markets — powered by ultra-fast execution and intelligent automation.

⚡ Key Features:
• Lightning-fast trade execution using advanced infrastructure
• Smart trade selection and structured opportunities
• Built-in risk management (stop-loss, take-profit, position sizing)
• Leverage options to amplify potential returns
• Clean, mobile-first interface via Telegram Mini App
• Demo mode to test strategies with zero risk

🤖 What makes RIFT different?
RIFT combines AI-driven analysis + automated execution, scanning multiple market opportunities and structuring them into simple, actionable trades — all within seconds.

You don’t need to be a professional trader or understand complex charts. RIFT does the heavy lifting, helping you participate in markets with speed, structure, and efficiency.

videos.title: Pac-12 preview reactions + could Brendan Sorsby & Texas Tech be the tipping point for CFB?
videos.summary_source_text:
Pac-12 preview reactions + could Brendan Sorsby & Texas Tech be the tipping point for CFB? Justin Michael begins the podcast by talking about Damon Washington in the latest edition of “Remember that Ram” before giving out the “CSU Stat of the Day” and a brief update on the latest commitments for CSU Football. He talks about Blaise Burwell choosing to be a two-sport athlete in Fort Collins and the significance of landing Todd Nordenson over Wyoming and multiple Pac-12 foes. Finally, Justin gets into Pac-12 projections from ESPN and Athlon. He gives his reactions to the national takes, plus talks about the questions he has for all of the teams going into 2026.

Plug a FREE N2 “Brain” Into Hermes Agent (1‑Tap Profiles, Shared Memory, Sub‑Agents)

The script explains how to add a new free model, NexAgi’s N2 Pro (built on Qwen 3.5), to Hermes Agent via OpenRouter, creating a dedicated Hermes profile that can be summoned in one tap and switched with other agent profiles. It shows the setup path (Manage → Models → Change → OpenRouter → N2 free) and highlights benefits like using a powerful agentic model for free, delegating work across multiple profiles and a Hermes Kanban board, and running long-horizon tasks in Hermes goal mode. It notes N2’s 262K context and that it won’t match top paid models, but is strong for agentic workflows, coding, tool use, research, and text/image input. Conversations log to an Obsidian memory vault and outputs save into an N2 workspace folder. The episode ends by promoting the AI Profit Boardroom “agent operating system” with shared memory, dashboard, coaching calls, and community.

videos.title: One Win Away For The Canes | Morning Cuppa Hockey
videos.summary_source_text:
One Win Away For The Canes | Morning Cuppa Hockey Season 3 Episode 147 of Morning Cuppa Hockey is LIVE with your host Jonny Lazarus and former NHL defenceman Colby Cohen, and the Stanley Cup is officially one win away from being hoisted after Carolina's massive 4-2 Game 5 victory in Raleigh. 

The Hurricanes now lead 3-2 with a chance to close it out in Vegas on Sunday, and the guys break it all down with a stacked lineup. 

Former NHL defenceman Mathieu Schneider joins the show to dig into Game 5, Brandon Bussi's incredible emergence, and what Carolina needs to do to finish the job on the road, plus his thoughts on the Mike Babcock investigation given his own history playing under Babcock in Detroit. 

Then NHL.com's Dan Rosen, who's been on the ground all series, gives an inside look at the mood in both locker rooms heading into a potential Cup-clinching Game 6. 

Finally, James van Riemsdyk stops by to talk Cup Final, his own Babcock connection in Toronto, and what it's like suiting up alongside Dylan Larkin in Detroit as trade rumours swirl around his teammate. 

Don't miss a second of the daily hockey talk!

🎙️ Subscribe to the Morning Cuppa Hockey podcast — Apple, Spotify, wherever you listen.

10.06.26 - RIFT Presentation 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

RIFT is a next-generation trading interface built on top of Hyperliquid, designed to simplify and accelerate how users interact with modern financial markets.

Instead of manually buying and selling assets, RIFT allows you to take positions on price movements (up or down) across crypto and other markets — powered by ultra-fast execution and intelligent automation.

⚡ Key Features:
• Lightning-fast trade execution using advanced infrastructure
• Smart trade selection and structured opportunities
• Built-in risk management (stop-loss, take-profit, position sizing)
• Leverage options to amplify potential returns
• Clean, mobile-first interface via Telegram Mini App
• Demo mode to test strategies with zero risk

🤖 What makes RIFT different?
RIFT combines AI-driven analysis + automated execution, scanning multiple market opportunities and structuring them into simple, actionable trades — all within seconds.

You don’t need to be a professional trader or understand complex charts. RIFT does the heavy lifting, helping you participate in markets with speed, structure, and efficiency.

📊 Built for the modern era of trading, where automation and algorithmic execution dominate over manual trading.

videos.title: Joe Sakic Confident In This Avalanche Team. Says Makar Will Play Entire Career With Franchise.
videos.summary_source_text:
Joe Sakic Confident In This Avalanche Team. Says Makar Will Play Entire Career With Franchise. Colorado Avalanche fans get major reassurance: Joe Sakic declares Cale Makar an “Avalanche for life.” With Makar’s contract extension talks already in motion, Sakic’s confidence in keeping this franchise defenseman squashes speculation and lifts concerns—are Avalanche supporters finally free from another Miko Rantanen-like saga?

Chris Micieli and Zach Pearson examine key takeaways from the Avalanche’s press conference, including Jared Bednar’s confirmed return, the coaching staff’s future, and the controversial decision to retain power play coach Dave Hakstol. The hosts dissect the team’s playoff shortcomings, debate the impact of aging stars like Nazem Kadri and Brock Nelson, and spotlight Gabe Landeskog’s remarkable comeback season. Plus, they break down the NHL’s Hart Trophy voting, questioning why Nathan MacKinnon and Colorado’s regular-season dominance weren’t reflected in awards season. Can this Avalanche core deliver another Stanley Cup—or is the window closing faster than expected?

The hosts dissect the team’s playoff shortcomings, debate the impact of aging stars like Nazem Kadri and Brock Nelson, and spotlight Gabe Landeskog’s remarkable comeback season. Plus, they break down the NHL’s Hart Trophy voting, questioning why Nathan MacKinnon and Colorado’s regular-season dominance weren’t reflected in awards season. Can this Avalanche core deliver another Stanley Cup—or is the window closing faster than expected?

videos.title: PGA TOUR Highlights | RBC Canadian Open | Round 1 | 2026
videos.summary_source_text:
PGA TOUR Highlights | RBC Canadian Open | Round 1 | 2026 Check out all the best shots from round 1 of the 2026 RBC Canadian Open, featuring Sahith Theegala, Brooks Koepka, Tony Finau and Tommy Fleetwood, among others.

The PGA TOUR returns north of the border this week for the RBC Canadian Open, one of the longest-running events on the schedule and the third-oldest championship on the PGA TOUR calendar. TPC Toronto at Osprey Valley’s North Course hosts the national open for the second consecutive year, welcoming one of the strongest fields in tournament history as players make their final preparations for the U.S. Open. Four of the world’s top 10 players are set to tee it up, led by Matt Fitzpatrick, Justin Rose, Tommy Fleetwood and Collin Morikawa, who returns to the event for the first time since 2019. Recent PGA Championship winner Aaron Rai is back in action, while Brooks Koepka makes his first RBC Canadian Open appearance since 2019. Viktor Hovland adds further intrigue in his tournament debut, joining a field that also includes Robert MacIntyre, Sam Burns, Shane Lowry, Wyndham Clark, Tony Finau and Max Homa. Defending champion Ryan Fox returns looking to become the first player since Jhonattan Vegas to successfully defend the title. Home hopes will once again be high, with Corey Conners, Taylor Pendrith, Mackenzie Hughes and rookie Sudarshan Yellamaraju leading a strong Canadian contingent. Mike Weir is also set for his 33rd appearance in Canada’s national championship. In addition to 500 FedExCup points, a place in next week’s U.S. Open is available for the leading player not already exempt, adding another layer of significance to an already stacked championship. With a world-class field and plenty at stake, the RBC Canadian Open is set for another memorable week at TPC Toronto.

videos.title: Brooks Koepka starts hot with 6-under 64 | RBC Canadian Open | 2026
videos.summary_source_text:
Brooks Koepka starts hot with 6-under 64 | RBC Canadian Open | 2026 In the opening round of the 2026 RBC Canadian Open, Brooks Koepka shoots a 6-under 64 to share a five-way tie for the lead after Thursday.

The PGA TOUR returns north of the border this week for the RBC Canadian Open, one of the longest-running events on the schedule and the third-oldest championship on the PGA TOUR calendar. TPC Toronto at Osprey Valley’s North Course hosts the national open for the second consecutive year, welcoming one of the strongest fields in tournament history as players make their final preparations for the U.S. Open. Four of the world’s top 10 players are set to tee it up, led by Matt Fitzpatrick, Justin Rose, Tommy Fleetwood and Collin Morikawa, who returns to the event for the first time since 2019. Recent PGA Championship winner Aaron Rai is back in action, while Brooks Koepka makes his first RBC Canadian Open appearance since 2019. Viktor Hovland adds further intrigue in his tournament debut, joining a field that also includes Robert MacIntyre, Sam Burns, Shane Lowry, Wyndham Clark, Tony Finau and Max Homa. Defending champion Ryan Fox returns looking to become the first player since Jhonattan Vegas to successfully defend the title. Home hopes will once again be high, with Corey Conners, Taylor Pendrith, Mackenzie Hughes and rookie Sudarshan Yellamaraju leading a strong Canadian contingent. Mike Weir is also set for his 33rd appearance in Canada’s national championship. In addition to 500 FedExCup points, a place in next week’s U.S. Open is available for the leading player not already exempt, adding another layer of significance to an already stacked championship. With a world-class field and plenty at stake, the RBC Canadian Open is set for another memorable week at TPC Toronto.

videos.title: The Fable 5 Backlash Is Getting Serious
videos.summary_source_text:
The Fable 5 Backlash Is Getting Serious Claude Fable 5 is already facing huge backlash, and the reason is bigger than simple safety filters. Users say Anthropic’s newest Claude model is refusing harmless prompts, triggering false alarms, and in some cases limiting advanced AI-related work in ways that were not clearly visible at first. Fable 5 may still be one of the most powerful public AI models ever released, but the controversy is now about trust: when Claude gives you an answer, are you getting the real Fable 5, a downgraded Opus fallback, or a weakened version shaped behind the scenes?

📩 Brand Deals & Partnerships: collabs@nouralabs.com
✉️ General Inquiries: airevolutionofficial@gmail.com
🚀 New Channel: https://www.youtube.com/@space.revolution

📌 What You’ll See:
The Register on Fable 5 blocking harmless prompts, including the “hello” example
SOURCE: https://www.theregister.com/ai-and-ml/2026/06/10/anthropic-claude-fable-5-refuses-innocuous-prompts/5253754
Business Insider on Anthropic admitting it made the wrong tradeoff with Fable 5 guardrails
SOURCE: https://www.businessinsider.com/anthropic-mythos-made-wrong-tradeoff-new-model-guardrails-llm-development-2026-6
The Verge on Anthropic apologizing for invisible Claude Fable guardrails
SOURCE: https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/948280/anthropic-claude-fable-invisible-distillation-guardrail
Anthropic’s official Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5 announcement
SOURCE: https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-fable-5-mythos-5
Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5 system card
SOURCE: https://www-cdn.anthropic.com/d00db56fa754a1b115b6dd7cb2e3c342ee809620.pdf

🚨 Why It Matters
Claude Fable 5 shows a new problem in frontier AI. The most powerful models are no longer judged only by how smart they are, but by how much users can trust what they are getting. If a model can refuse harmless work, fall back to a weaker model, or limit certain answers behind the scenes, the real question becomes whether closed AI systems are becoming too controlled to fully trust.

videos.title: How Breathing Influences Your Nervous System
videos.summary_source_text:
How Breathing Influences Your Nervous System The body doesn't always know the difference between a real threat and a stressful thought. Modern life keeps many people in a constant state of tension, with elevated stress signals running throughout the day. The good news is that the nervous system can be influenced intentionally. Something as simple as slowing the breath and extending the exhale can activate the vagus nerve and help restore a healthier balance between stress and recovery. This is around 20 years ago I starting using the RESPeRATE device with my patients. You can learn more about it and receive a special discount by using the link in my linktree that is linked on my channel page.

videos.title: How To Fix Hockey Issues Faster
videos.summary_source_text:
How To Fix Hockey Issues Faster The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

👉  Drills, breakdowns, progressions, and full mechanics playbook (100's of videos) all available in Train 2.0+ here: https://app.train2point0.com/bundles/membership-plus

Plus, what did we learn from the Denver Broncos’ practice on Thursday? Que Robinson, Jonah Elliss and the rest of the pass rush beat up on Jarrett Stidham and Sam Ehlinger. The young receivers made some plays, too.

videos.title: Sean Payton's HUGE Contract Extension, Jonathon Cooper, Davis Webb & More Inside Broncos OTAs
videos.summary_source_text:
Sean Payton's HUGE Contract Extension, Jonathon Cooper, Davis Webb & More Inside Broncos OTAs Sean Payton signs a huge Denver Broncos contract extension, locking in the future of the Broncos alongside George Paton, Bo Nix and ownership after back-to-back playoff runs. We break down everything from Week 2 of Broncos OTAs, including Bo Nix’s injury update, Davis Webb’s first comments as offensive coordinator, Jonathon Cooper’s situation and Jaylen Waddle’s eye-opening speed at practice. Plus, Vance Joseph discusses the Broncos defense, Q Robinson’s rise, Ja’Quan McMillian’s future, Jeremy Crawshaw’s booming punts, and key standouts like Pat Bryant and Evan Engram. Get all the latest Denver Broncos news, OTA takeaways and updates before mandatory minicamp and training camp. Zac Stevens goes inside Broncos Practice for the latest updates & news for Broncos Country.

videos.title: Dylan Larkin Trade Sweepstakes EXPANDS, New Teams Emerge | DFO Rundown Insider Edition
videos.summary_source_text:
Dylan Larkin Trade Sweepstakes EXPANDS, New Teams Emerge | DFO Rundown Insider Edition The Stanley Cup Final may still be rolling on, but NHL offseason is already in full swing. Irfaan Gaffar and David Pagnotta break down the growing speculation surrounding Connor Hellebuyck's future in Winnipeg, including reports that the Jets star may be open to a move. The guys discuss why the Florida Panthers continue to be linked to the Vezina winner, what a potential trade package could look like, and how a deal of that magnitude could reshape the entire goaltending market.

They also dive into the latest on Dylan Larkin's trade situation, Alex Tuch's contract situation with the Buffalo Sabres, Owen Power's surprising appearance in trade chatter, and the coaching searches underway in Toronto and Edmonton. 

#NHL #DailyFaceoff #ConnorHellebuyck #DylanLarkin #TorontoMapleLeafs #BuffaloSabres #FloridaPanthers #NHLRumours #StanleyCupFinal #HockeyNews

🕐 CHAPTERS
0:00 - Intro
0:45 - CBJ goaltending situation
2:15 - Connor Hellebuyck
6:45 - Player empowerment
8:32 - Dylan Larkin
9:34 - Alex Tuch
11:18 - Owen Power
13:38 - Coaching updates

videos.title: Stanley Cup Final Game 5 Preview: Hurricanes Return Home in Pivotal Matchup
videos.summary_source_text:
Stanley Cup Final Game 5 Preview: Hurricanes Return Home in Pivotal Matchup The Stanley Cup Final returns to Carolina for a pivotal Game 5 as the Carolina Hurricanes and Vegas Golden Knights enter a best-of-three series tied at 2-2. With momentum swinging after Carolina's Game 4 victory, all eyes turn to Lenovo Center where one team will move just one win away from lifting hockey's ultimate prize.

Can the Hurricanes continue to ride the incredible play of Jordan Staal and their depth scoring, or will Vegas get the offensive breakthrough they're looking for from Jack Eichel in the biggest game of the season? We break down the key storylines, goaltending questions, matchups to watch, X-factors, and what each team needs to do to take control of the series heading into Game 6.

With recent history showing how important Game 5 can be in a tied Stanley Cup Final, this matchup has all the ingredients of an instant classic. Who has the edge tonight? We preview everything you need to know before puck drop.

#Hurricanes #VegasGoldenKnights #StanleyCupFinal #NHLPlayoffs

videos.title: How To Play Par-3s Smarter #golfswing #golftips
videos.summary_source_text:
How To Play Par-3s Smarter #golfswing #golftips A lot of par-3 shots come up short before the swing even starts. When you tee the ball too high, you lose ball speed and don’t let the loft of the club do the work. Add in aiming straight at tucked pins and choosing clubs based on your best strike, and you’re setting yourself up for failure. In this video we show how better tee height, smarter targets, and realistic club choice lead to more greens hit.
Follow for more practical golf tips from Me and My Golf. A lot of par-3 shots come up short before the swing even starts. videos.title: IF YOU CAN’T DO THIS, YOUR BODY MAY BE AGING FASTER THAN YOU THINK | Dr. Mandell
videos.summary_source_text:
IF YOU CAN’T DO THIS, YOUR BODY MAY BE AGING FASTER THAN YOU THINK | Dr. Mandell Can a simple 30-second chair test reveal how well your body is aging? In this video, Dr. Mandell explains why the ability to stand up from a chair without using your hands is one of the most powerful indicators of lower body strength, balance, mobility, and long-term independence. Your glutes and leg muscles are essential for preventing falls, supporting your spine, improving posture, protecting your joints, and maintaining an active lifestyle as you age. The good news is that it's never too late to improve. Learn how this easy daily exercise can strengthen your body, build confidence, and help you stay healthier, stronger, and more independent for years to come. Small actions performed consistently can lead to life-changing results. Challenge yourself today and start investing in a stronger future.

*********************************

Please share this video with your friends and family.  Wishing you and your family many Blessings!  ❤️😊🙏 Dr M

Your glutes and leg muscles are essential for preventing falls, supporting your spine, improving posture, protecting your joints, and maintaining an active lifestyle as you age. The good news is that it's never too late to improve. Learn how this easy daily exercise can strengthen your body, build confidence, and help you stay healthier, stronger, and more independent for years to come. Small actions performed consistently can lead to life-changing results. Challenge yourself today and start investing in a stronger future.

videos.title: Cup Final Game 5 Tonight & Babcock Investigation Continues ft. David Pagnotta & Greg Wyshynski
videos.summary_source_text:
Cup Final Game 5 Tonight & Babcock Investigation Continues ft. David Pagnotta & Greg Wyshynski The Stanley Cup Final is down to a best-of-three, and Game 5 takes center stage tonight in Carolina as the Hurricanes and Vegas Golden Knights battle for a crucial edge with the series tied 2-2. On today's edition of The Sheet, Jeff Marek and Greg Wyshynski break down the biggest storylines heading into Game 5, including key matchups, lineup decisions, standout performers, and what each team must do to take control of the series. Plus, David Pagnotta joins the show to discuss the latest developments surrounding the Edmonton Oilers' pursuit of Mike Babcock, the fallout from Dylan Larkin's trade request out of Detroit, whether more members of Team USA's gold medal-winning roster could be on the move this summer, and the latest NHL offseason news and rumours from around the league. It's another packed MVSW edition of The Sheet with Jeff Marek and Greg Wyshynski as the Stanley Cup Final reaches a pivotal turning point.

videos.title: Llama 4 Explained: The 10M Token AI Meta Gave Away for Free
videos.summary_source_text:
Llama 4 Explained: The 10M Token AI Meta Gave Away for Free Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Meta just dropped Llama 4 — and instead of locking it behind an API, they gave it away for free. In this video, I break down exactly what Llama 4 is, how the mixture-of-experts architecture works, what Scout, Maverick, and Behemoth actually do, and whether the 10 million token context window is as game-changing as it sounds.

We'll look at the real benchmark picture (including where Llama 4 still trails OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google), the licensing terms that make it free for commercial use, and whether open-source AI is genuinely closing the gap — or just closing in on the hype.

⏱️ CHAPTERS
00:00 – Why Llama 4 Changes Everything
01:09 – The Origin Story
02:46 – The Technical Breakdown
04:37 – The Competitive Landscape
06:04 – The Real-World Picture
07:38 – The Verdict

So — are you team open-source, or do you still trust the closed models? Drop your take in the comments.

The creator shows features like wake-word listening, conversation history, wall mode, diagnostics such as “show me my numbers,” and viewing a team of agents. The system is powered by Claude Desktop in the background and uses ElevenLabs for voice, with ongoing improvements and multilingual/joke capabilities. videos.title: GPT-5.5 Review: What OpenAI's "Super App" Model Actually Does
videos.summary_source_text:
GPT-5.5 Review: What OpenAI's "Super App" Model Actually Does Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
OpenAI's GPT-5.5 — codenamed "Spud" — launched on April 23, 2026, and it's being positioned as a step toward what Greg Brockman calls an AI "super app." But how much of that is real, and how much is it just marketing dressed up as a product launch?

In this deep dive, I go through everything that actually matters about GPT-5.5: the technical specs under the hood, the benchmark results that prove (and disprove) OpenAI's claims, and the honest competitive picture against Claude Opus 4.7 and Gemini 3.1 Pro. No hype, no hand-waving — just what the model is genuinely good at, where it falls short, and how to actually get value out of it whether you're paying or on the free tier.

We cover the 1.05 million token context window, the new reasoning effort setting, the multimodal inputs most users still aren't touching, the Terminal-Bench 2.0 and Expert-SWE benchmark numbers, the fantasy creature reinforcement learning quirk that fast-tracked GPT-5.6, and the GPT-5.5 Instant rollout for free-tier ChatGPT users.

If you want an evidence-first read on whether GPT-5.5 is worth your time — and which model is actually right for your work — this is the breakdown.

⏱️ Chapters
00:00 The setting most users are missing
1:36 The backstory: how GPT-5.5 came to be
3:48 The technical specs: what's actually under the hood
6:48 The benchmark results: where GPT-5.5 actually wins
9:20 The competition: how it stacks up against Claude and Gemini
11:43 The use cases: what GPT-5.5 is actually built for
14:02 The versions and pricing: what you actually get
16:32 The verdict

🔔 Subscribe for skepticism-led AI coverage that separates real capability from vendor claims.

💬 Which GPT-5.5 feature are you most excited to actually use? Drop it in the comments.

videos.title: Stanley Cup Final Swing Game & Jeff Marek Joins | Daily Faceoff Live
videos.summary_source_text:
Stanley Cup Final Swing Game & Jeff Marek Joins | Daily Faceoff Live Game 5 of the Stanley Cup Final has arrived, and with the series tied, tonight could be the ultimate swing game. We also fire up another edition of Summer Matchmaker, finding the best landing spots for Brendan Gallagher, Elias Pettersson, and Patrik Laine as offseason rumours continue to heat up.

videos.title: I Test Golf Clubs I Never Unboxed (CRAZY CLUBS!)
videos.summary_source_text:
I Test Golf Clubs I Never Unboxed (CRAZY CLUBS!) Rick has no idea what's inside these mystery golf club boxes... and neither do you. From brand-new releases to forgotten gems (and maybe a few disasters), every box contains a complete surprise. Which clubs will impress, and which belong straight back in the box? Let's find out.

Welcome to H.I.T Golf Reviews – the home of honest, independent and trustworthy golf equipment reviews.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.

Our testing is built around launch monitor data (GCQuad) and real-world shots, but we always explain the numbers in normal-golfer language. No fluff, no hidden agendas – just whether a club will help your game or not.

Whether you’re:
buying your first proper set,
a mid-handicapper looking for more forgiveness, or
a low-handicap player chasing that perfect combo of feel and distance,

H.I.T is here to help you pick the right gear for your swing, not just the newest thing on the shelf.

Why H.I.T exists:
To cut through the hype.
To give golfers the truth.
To save you time, money and a few golf balls.
To make the game a bit easier – and more fun.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 — What's Real, What's Marketing, and When It's Worth the Price
videos.summary_source_text:
Claude Fable 5 — What's Real, What's Marketing, and When It's Worth the Price Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Every few weeks a lab ships a model, calls it "the most capable yet," and leaves you squinting at a benchmark chart wondering if anything changed. So I read Anthropic's launch post, the developer notes, and the fine print almost nobody reads. The real story behind Claude Fable 5 isn't a smarter model — it's that Anthropic just handed you a public cut of a tier it previously said was too risky to release.

This is the evidence-first breakdown: what Fable 5 (and its locked-down twin Mythos 5) actually is, where Anthropic's claims hold up, where they earn an asterisk, and exactly when Fable is worth the premium versus when you're better off on something cheaper.

⏱️ CHAPTERS
0:00 Intro
0:56 What Anthropic Actually Shipped
2:31 What's Genuinely New Versus What's Marketing
3:45 The Wow Examples — and the Asterisks
5:07 The Real Headline: A Mythos-Class Model Goes Public
6:07 The Interface: Models and Effort, Decoded
7:34 Always-On Thinking and Why Fable Feels Different
8:23 How to Actually Get Value Out of It
9:33 The Safeguards, and Why Fable Hands Off to Opus
10:45 The Catch Nobody Puts on the Slide
11:51 The Price and the Honest Recommendation
12:50 Why This Launch Matters, and Where We Go Next

A few things to keep your skeptic hat on for: nearly all the headline benchmarks are Anthropic measuring Anthropic, the "months into days" examples are vendor-surfaced testimonials, the new tokenizer can count ~30% more tokens for the same text, and "1M context" is API/Claude Code only — not ordinary consumer chat.

Next video: Fable 5 head-to-head against Opus 4.8 on the same prompts, with the costs shown. Subscribe so you don't miss it.

👇 Drop a comment: what do you actually use Fable for, and where have you kept it on a cheaper model?

videos.title: Gemini Spark Is Google’s Most Powerful AI Tool Yet
videos.summary_source_text:
Gemini Spark Is Google’s Most Powerful AI Tool Yet *Gemini at Work Guide:* https://clickhubspot.com/8f8u

Google just released Gemini Spark, a personal AI agent. In this video, I walk through how I use Google Gemini Spark to run AI tasks in the background, even when my laptop is off. Gemini Spark is a new AI agent inside the Gemini app, and it can work with Gmail, Google Drive, Google Sheets, Google Calendar, and more.

I show how Gemini Spark can scan my emails, read AI newsletters, find key ideas, and turn that research into content. I also show how I use Gemini Spark skills to match my writing style, so the AI can write more like me.

Then I go step by step through scheduled tasks. I show how Gemini Spark can check my inbox every week, send me a recap, update a Google Sheet, and create a Google Drive folder from a new email. I also test how it can look at my Google Calendar and build a meeting brief from my emails and Drive files.

This is still an early and experimental Google AI tool, so I also talk about privacy and why you should be careful with what you connect. Gemini Spark can be very useful, but it also needs access to a lot of your Google account.

This video is for anyone who wants to understand Gemini Spark, Google Gemini AI agents, AI automation, Gmail automation, Google Workspace automation, and how AI skills can make these tools more helpful. *Gemini at Work Guide:* https://clickhubspot.com/8f8u

Pivotal Game 5 & Off-Ice Drama | Morning Cuppa Hockey Season 3, Episode 146 of Morning Cuppa Hockey is here and Jonny Lazarus and former NHL defenceman Colby Cohen are bringing you a loaded Cup Final morning. 

Former Carolina Hurricane Erik Cole joins the show fresh off the Canes' massive 5-3 Game 4 win over the Vegas Golden Knights to break down how the series has shifted, what Brandon Bussi's playoff debut says about this team's depth and culture, the goaltending decision looming for Rod Brind'Amour heading into Game 5, and how Logan Stankoven has emerged as one of the most dangerous forwards left standing in the playoffs. 

Then, The Fourth Period's David Pagnotta drops the latest news and notes from around the league — including the NHL's investigation into Mike Babcock and what it actually means for Edmonton's pursuit of the veteran coach, where the Dylan Larkin trade situation stands heading into the draft, and what else is quietly heating up as the offseason creeps closer.

When these nutrients get depleted, you may notice symptoms like anxiety, insomnia, nervous tension, heart palpitations, mood changes, excessive worrying, muscle spasms, and even skin problems.

videos.title: Why Your Legs Won’t Stop Moving at Night (Fix ASAP)
videos.summary_source_text:
Why Your Legs Won’t Stop Moving at Night (Fix ASAP) Do you have restless legs at night? Discover the underlying cause of restless leg syndrome, how to stop getting leg cramps at night, and how to fix the sleep problems caused by restless legs within 72 hours.

0:00 Restless legs at night
0:54 What causes restless legs syndrome? 
1:14 Dopamine and restless legs syndrome
1:56 Iron and nerve health 
2:30 Why you have restless legs at night 
3:13 Mineral deficiencies and restless legs syndrome
5:25 More restless legs syndrome causes
6:34 What causes the mineral deficiencies related to restless legs syndrome?
7:20 Restless legs relief 

🌟 10 biological signals control how you feel every day, but only 1 matters most for YOUR body. Take the free 2-minute quiz to find out: https://drbrg.co/3RUZk2J

Learn more about common nutrient deficiencies, what may be causing them, and how to correct them naturally: 
▶️ https://youtu.be/Uw21AZN7Ulc
▶️ https://youtu.be/7lSQ2hnycLg
▶️ https://youtu.be/89jwTIbWRuI

For a deeper look at restless legs syndrome and what may be causing it, check out this article: https://drbrg.co/4uonEHx

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

Restless legs at night are often described as a buildup of energy in the thigh muscles that can make it difficult to relax and sleep.

Restless legs syndrome is not actually a problem with the legs; it’s a neurological issue involving the brain. Many people are treated with medications that affect dopamine, but these drugs may eventually stop working and often worsen the problem. 

Restless legs syndrome is often linked to low iron, but simply taking iron doesn’t usually solve the problem. Nerves need both iron and copper in the proper amounts, and iron cannot function correctly without copper.

Symptoms of restless legs syndrome tend to worsen at night because dopamine follows a circadian rhythm and reaches its lowest point during the evening. A subclinical dopamine deficiency can become much more noticeable at night. 

ATP is the critical fuel source for neurons, and ATP production depends on magnesium. Magnesium helps regulate calcium in the muscles by driving it out of muscle cells so they can relax. Without enough magnesium, muscles can remain tight.

Magnesium deficiency can contribute to restless legs, sleep problems, muscle cramps, and reduced ATP production in the nervous system.

Vitamin B1 is also essential for preventing restless legs at night. Vitamin B1 deficiency is associated with peripheral neuropathy, anxiety, nervous tension, and impaired dopamine production.

A high-carbohydrate diet can deplete copper, magnesium, and vitamin B1, potentially contributing to restless legs syndrome symptoms.

videos.title: Unpopular opinion: you don't need to ask ChatGPT everything
videos.summary_source_text:
Unpopular opinion: you don't need to ask ChatGPT everything This might be an unpopular opinion, but most people don't need to use cloud AI models for everything.

Most people are using the most state-of-the-art AI models from ChatGPT or Claude to summarize documents or write emails for them, and frankly these models have gotten too smart for that. You don't need all the compute for everyday tasks. 

Running local models directly on your computer means you don't need the internet, your data is completely private, and you're not at the whims of the big AI labs. 

With amazing new local models and even better hardware, your computer can handle 90% of the simple things we ask AI to do.

Would you switch to using a local AI model?

videos.title: Shocking New Toyota – I didn’t see this coming!
videos.summary_source_text:
Shocking New Toyota – I didn’t see this coming! Head to 16:13 to see Mat in the mid-engined car, and then later drive the first Celica prototype!

Mat’s teamed up with Toyota in Japan to get hands-on with the M Concept, attend a 24-hour endurance race with the Gazoo Racing team, and even have an exclusive look at the all-new Corolla GRMN!

Explore new and used car deals with Carwow: https://bit.ly/Change-Your-Car-1106

Toyota insiders have dropped massive hints that the mid-engine M Concept prototype will likely spawn the next-generation Celica! It features a brand-new, mid-mounted 2.0-litre four-cylinder turbo with over 400hp, and unlike the previous generation of Celica, it’ll come with all-wheel drive! The concept has been developed under Gazoo Racing's ultimate philosophy of "test, break, fix, repeat," and Toyota is currently openly racing this unfinished prototype to push its limits. 

With that in mind, Mat’s joined the team on the grid at Fuji Speedway to witness the Gazoo Racing team testing the concept in a gruelling 24-hour endurance race. Alongside the M Concept, GR is racing a standard 1.6-litre GR Yaris to test biofuel, prototype braking systems, and advanced swan-neck aero packages! 

While at the track, Mat also catches up with Toyota's legendary chairman, Akio Toyoda. In a completely unscripted conversation, Akio explains why he chooses fun over corporate bureaucracy, his love for the smell and sound of real engines, and reveals that the cult-classic GR Yaris might have actually turned over a small profit!

Mat also had a chance to get hands-on with the newly unveiled Toyota Corolla GRMN, and there’s absolutely loads to love about it! Torque is up to 450Nm, the rear seats have been ripped out (shedding 30kg), and racing bucket seats have been added up front! It also has a carbon fibre bonnet and spoiler. Unfortunately, though, it’s not coming to the UK! 😢

Finally, Mat joins Toyota at the Shimoyama test facility to test drive the various concepts and prototypes. From a terrifying passenger lap in the 400hp M Concept to sliding a 1.6-litre mid-engine concept on a loose gravel rally stage, this video isn’t one you want to miss! 

The question is, who wants to see Toyota resurrect the Celica? Let us know in the comments! Head to 16:13 to see Mat in the mid-engined car, and then later drive the first Celica prototype!

If you're a high handicap golfer struggling with thin iron shots, no divot, and no compression — this is your problem too. In this golf lesson, Eric Cogorno shows exactly why the fix isn't at impact. It starts with the takeaway. Billy's club was getting too far low and inside due to a lack of vertical wrist hinge. Eric walks through the exact golf drills used to fix Billy's golf swing, improve his swing plane, and get him compressing the golf ball. These are practical golf tips any high handicap golfer can apply to start hitting better iron shots and learn how to play golf at a higher level.

What You'll Learn:
• Why a lack of vertical wrist hinge causes an under-plane takeaway and poor iron contact
• How to use the Swing Coach App to identify golf swing problems in real time
• How to increase wrist hinge progressively during the backswing for better compression
• The butt-of-club and shaft-parallel checkpoint for correct early hinge
• The thud the mat drill for consistent ground interaction and divots
• How the 3:00 and 2:00 station golf drill keeps the club on plane through the takeaway and downswing
• Why high handicap golfers who are too far inside must exaggerate to find neutral swing plane
• How handle position at setup pre-sets swing plane and wrist hinge
• How to compress the golf ball and stop hitting thin iron shots for good

videos.title: Larkin, Babcock, and the Cup Final | GS Off Ice SPECIAL Off Season Edition
videos.summary_source_text:
Larkin, Babcock, and the Cup Final | GS Off Ice SPECIAL Off Season Edition The Stanley Cup Final is four games deep, and we're breaking down everything we've seen from the series so far, including our updated predictions, biggest surprises, and whether momentum has shifted heading into the final stretch. We also dive into the latest Dylan Larkin trade rumors, with reports linking the Red Wings captain to Florida, Minnesota, and Vegas. Which destination makes the most sense, which teams need him the most, and what could Detroit realistically expect in return for a player with four years remaining on his contract?

We also discuss the Edmonton Oilers' decision to hire Mike Babcock as their next head coach, whether coaching was truly the team's biggest issue, and what Babcock brings to the table that Kris Knoblauch didn't. Plus, we react to the latest NHL Awards, including the Jack Adams, Norris, Ted Lindsay, and Vezina Trophy winners, before making our own predictions for who will take home the Hart Trophy.

In this week's Money Moves segment, we examine the Minnesota Wild's six-year extension for Michael McCarron and what it means for their salary cap situation moving forward, as well as Evgeni Malkin's new one-year deal to remain in Pittsburgh. Would we have made the same decisions if we were running those organizations?

Finally, we continue our offseason Team In Review series with the Vancouver Canucks, Chicago Blackhawks, and Calgary Flames, identifying the biggest priorities each franchise must address this summer. We wrap things up with another edition of Flaming Hot Takes, where we each bring two bold NHL predictions and put them to the test.

videos.title: HOME SWEET HOME! Carolina Hurricanes Seeking to Take 3-2 Series Lead Back Home in Game 5
videos.summary_source_text:
HOME SWEET HOME! Carolina Hurricanes Seeking to Take 3-2 Series Lead Back Home in Game 5 The Carolina Hurricanes are seeking to take a 3-2 series lead after Thursday night's Game 5. What are the big storylines heading into Game 5 back in Raleigh as the Hurricanes look to take their first lead of the Stanley Cup Final? Who needs to step up, and who should we watch from the Hurricanes to continue their impact? On the Thursday Edition of Locked On Hurricanes, Zach begins the show by sharing his thoughts on the goalie situation once again, but this time for Game 5. While Rod Brind'Amour stated that they wanted to give Frederik Andersen more rest to be ready as their possible starter, should they go back to Brandon Bussi? After his performance in Game 4, does it make sense to hop back on the Bus, or go with the goalie that got them to the Stanley Cup Final? Secondly, Zach shares his thoughts on how the depth once again will need to be relied on for Game 5, just like the rest of the series. How can the Hurricanes benefit once again with depth scoring, and how do they need a full team effort? Finally, Zach concludes the show by picking his Canes to Click, of who will score for the Hurricanes in Game 5. Also, who are the players to watch? All of that and more, on the latest edition of Locked On Hurricanes.

On the Thursday Edition of Locked On Hurricanes, Zach begins the show by sharing his thoughts on the goalie situation once again, but this time for Game 5. While Rod Brind'Amour stated that they wanted to give Frederik Andersen more rest to be ready as their possible starter, should they go back to Brandon Bussi? After his performance in Game 4, does it make sense to hop back on the Bus, or go with the goalie that got them to the Stanley Cup Final? Secondly, Zach shares his thoughts on how the depth once again will need to be relied on for Game 5, just like the rest of the series. How can the Hurricanes benefit once again with depth scoring, and how do they need a full team effort? Finally, Zach concludes the show by picking his Canes to Click, of who will score for the Hurricanes in Game 5. Also, who are the players to watch? All of that and more, on the latest edition of Locked On Hurricanes.

videos.summary_source_text:
Texas Stars Captain Curtis McKenzie IS Returning For His 14th Season | DLLS Stars Podcast Take advantage of our Playoff Diehard Sale and join our community today! https://alldlls.com/stars-playoff-offer-youtube 

Former Dallas Star and current Texas Stars captain Curtis McKenzie joins the show today! We will talk to Curtis about his decision to sign another one-year American Hockey League contract with Texas, returning for his 14th professional season, his 11th with the Stars organization. McKenzie was drafted by Dallas in the sixth round of the 2009 NHL Entry Draft and has appeared in 99 career NHL games, all with the Stars, along with 725 career AHL games and has over 500 career AHL points. He also won the 2014 Calder Cup with Texas during his rookie season, where he won the 2013-14 Dudley “Red” Garrett Memorial Award as the AHL’s Most Outstanding Rookie. We will also get a What The Puck breakdown from Lud on last night’s Stanley Cup Final Game 4, as Carolina won 5-3 to even the series at 2-2 and touch on some NHL News & Notes, time permitting.

videos.summary_source_text:
Revisiting The Texas Rangers’ Offseason Moves As Gore Faces Royals Wednesday | DLLS Rangers Podcast Take advantage of our Playoff Diehard Sale and join our community today! https://alldlls.com/stars-playoff-offer-youtube 

An ALLCITY Network Production

The Texas Rangers made two big splashes in the offseason, first with trading Marcus Semien for Brandon Nimmo and later sending five prospects to the Nationals for left-hander MacKenzie Gore. Nimmo has been fine, better than his numbers show, but Gore has been up and down and in some fans’ crosshairs. We will look back at all of the Rangers’ offseason moves, but take a deeper look at Gore as he prepares to face Kansas City on Tuesday night. We’ll also take a look at the Vandy Boys, Kumar Rocker and Jack Leiter.

videos.title: 10.06.26 - RIFT Training
videos.summary_source_text:
10.06.26 - RIFT Training 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

RIFT is a next-generation trading interface built on top of Hyperliquid, designed to simplify and accelerate how users interact with modern financial markets.

Instead of manually buying and selling assets, RIFT allows you to take positions on price movements (up or down) across crypto and other markets — powered by ultra-fast execution and intelligent automation.

⚡ Key Features:
• Lightning-fast trade execution using advanced infrastructure
• Smart trade selection and structured opportunities
• Built-in risk management (stop-loss, take-profit, position sizing)
• Leverage options to amplify potential returns
• Clean, mobile-first interface via Telegram Mini App
• Demo mode to test strategies with zero risk

🤖 What makes RIFT different?
RIFT combines AI-driven analysis + automated execution, scanning multiple market opportunities and structuring them into simple, actionable trades — all within seconds.

videos.title: Skatterna och valet - Moderaternas Marie Nicholson
videos.summary_source_text:
Skatterna och valet - Moderaternas Marie Nicholson Moderaterna vill sänka tillväxthämmande skatter och stärka företagandet 

Sjätte intervjun i serien är med Moderaternas skattepolitiska talesperson Marie Nicholson, som intervjuas av PwC:s skatteexpert Ulrika Lundh Eriksson. I fokus står sänkning av tillväxthämmande skatter för att stärka företagande och konkurrenskraft. 

Marie Nicholson lyfter att Moderaterna vill sänka skatt på arbete och företagande, sänka bolagsskatten, på sikt till en av de lägsta i EU samt sänka energiskatterna. Partiet har tillväxt för ögonen och vill ha en skattemix som ger mer pengar i plånboken och möjlighet för företag att växa. Moderaterna säger nej till fastighetsskatt, arvs- och gåvoskatt samt förmögenhetsskatt. Moderaterna vill sänka tillväxthämmande skatter och stärka företagandet

videos.title: The Avalanche Are Still the Class of the Central, But Could We See The Division Get Shaken Up?
videos.summary_source_text:
The Avalanche Are Still the Class of the Central, But Could We See The Division Get Shaken Up? Colorado Avalanche set the bar high with early Central Division predictions—will their rivals keep up or shake the standings with bold offseason moves? Chris Micieli and Zach Pearson debate the fates of the Dallas Stars, Minnesota Wild, Winnipeg Jets, Nashville Predators, and more, breaking down salary cap crunches, standout talents like Connor Bedard and Clayton Keller, and the real impact of upcoming trades and free agent signings.

Key topics cover the Avalanche’s staying power in the Central, whether Chicago Blackhawks or St. Louis Blues remain at rock bottom, and which teams—like Utah and Dallas—could surprise or disappoint. The hosts assess the challenges Dallas faces retaining Jason Robertson, Nashville’s upside with Chris McFarland steering the ship, and Minnesota’s path to the top two. Plus, an honest review of Joel Kiviranta’s season spotlights the balance between depth and production.

Claude Fable 5: Mythos AI Goes Public Want the full playbook? Join the Creator Magic community 👉 https://mrc.fm/premium

Claude Fable 5 is the closest you can get to Anthropic's locked Mythos 5... and I've been running it. Same core model, public safety rails, and it genuinely thinks like a senior engineer.

In this one I put it to work on real builds. End to end coding, system design, and debugging the stuff that normally eats my whole day. It plans, verifies, and refines on its own, holds a million token context, and keeps going for days without me babysitting it.

I'll walk you through the exact workflows, the API setup, and the agent patterns I use to get an unfair edge as a solo builder.

videos.title: Hockey Blade Profile Preservation:  Elite vs the Competition
videos.summary_source_text:
Hockey Blade Profile Preservation:  Elite vs the Competition In this video, Chase with IW sits down with JP and Brian at Elite Blade Performance Technology to explain how Elite sharpening machines preserve the geometry of your skate blades with precision accuracy every time. While manual hand sharpening machines and other automated systems can gradually alter a custom profile, Elite's teaching pass maps the shape of the blade before sharpening and removes a consistent amount of steel from the heel, toe, and center.

Preserving that profile helps players maintain the speed, balance, and smooth turning performance they expect every time they step onto the ice.

In this video, we cover:
✅ Why preserving your custom blade profile is so important
✅ How Elite's teaching pass maps the blade before every sharpening
✅ How Elite removes a consistent amount of steel across the heel, toe, and center
✅ Why manual sharpening and other automated systems can gradually change blade geometry
✅ The performance issues caused by reverse-smile deformation and banana blades
✅ How to inspect your runners at home and recognize when it may be time for new steel

videos.title: How Elite’s E-S4 Sharpener is Different
videos.summary_source_text:
How Elite’s E-S4 Sharpener is Different In this video, Chase with IW teams up with Brian at Elite Blade Performance to break down why the Elite E-S4 Automated Skate Sharpener is the new benchmark in skate sharpening. Chase and Brian walk through the key E-S4 designs that deliver unmatched consistency, blade preservation, and pro-level results every single time.

Whether you're a pro shop owner, equipment manager, or hockey player in search of the very best sharpening, this is the video for you.

In this video, we cover:
✅ Consistent results every sharpening, same feel every time you hit the ice

videos.title: Kaprizov Uses This Move 14 Times To Dominate The Entire Shift
videos.summary_source_text:
Kaprizov Uses This Move 14 Times To Dominate The Entire Shift The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

videos.title: This NEW Driver Test Revealed A LOT MORE Than I Thought...
videos.summary_source_text:
This NEW Driver Test Revealed A LOT MORE Than I Thought... In this video, I put some of the most popular golf drivers through a test that every golfer should see. If you're searching for the most forgiving driver, the best driver of 2026, or simply want more distance and consistency off the tee, the results may surprise you.

Too often golfers choose a driver based on marketing, tour usage, or launch monitor numbers alone. This test reveals what really matters when it comes to forgiveness, accuracy, dispersion, and real-world performance on the golf course.

Which driver is the most forgiving? Which driver performs best under pressure? And is the latest technology actually making golfers better?

Watch until the end to see the full results and find out which driver came out on top.

videos.title: DON’T GO TO YOUR NEXT DOCTOR’S APPOINTMENT UNTIL YOU WATCH THIS | Dr. Mandell
videos.summary_source_text:
DON’T GO TO YOUR NEXT DOCTOR’S APPOINTMENT UNTIL YOU WATCH THIS | Dr. Mandell If you’re over 50, your next doctor’s appointment could be one of the most important opportunities to take control of your health—but only if you know the right questions to ask and how to prepare. In this video, I’ll share practical strategies to help you get the most value from every visit, including understanding your blood work, reviewing medications and supplements, discussing muscle health, balance, sleep, memory, and preventive care, and learning how to become an active participant in your own healthcare. Too often, people leave their appointments without the information they truly need to make better decisions about their future health. By asking thoughtful questions and understanding what really matters, you can work with your doctor to build a personalized plan that supports healthy aging, longevity, strength, independence, and a better quality of life. Whether you’re preparing for your annual physical or a routine follow-up, these simple steps can help you leave your appointment feeling informed, empowered, and ready to take charge of your health.


*********************************

Please share this video with your friends and family.  Wishing you and your family many Blessings!  ❤️😊🙏 Dr M

YouTube:     https://www.youtube.com/user/motivationaldoc 
Facebook:   https://www.facebook.com/motivationaldoc/
Instagram:  https://www.instagram.com/motivationaldoc/ If you’re over 50, your next doctor’s appointment could be one of the most important opportunities to take control of your health—but only if you know the right questions to ask and how to prepare. In this video, I’ll share practical strategies to help you get the most value from every visit, including understanding your blood work, reviewing medications and supplements, discussing muscle health, balance, sleep, memory, and preventive care, and learning how to become an active participant in your own healthcare. Too often, people leave their appointments without the information they truly need to make better decisions about their future health. By asking thoughtful questions and understanding what really matters, you can work with your doctor to build a personalized plan that supports healthy aging, longevity, strength, independence, and a better quality of life. Whether you’re preparing for your annual physical or a routine follow-up, these simple steps can help you leave your appointment feeling informed, empowered, and ready to take charge of your health.


*********************************

Please share this video with your friends and family.  Wishing you and your family many Blessings!  ❤️😊🙏 Dr M

By swinging halfway back, halfway through, and holding the finish, you train better sequencing and keep the clubface square. videos.title: When to Tight Turn and When to Punch Turn?
videos.summary_source_text:
When to Tight Turn and When to Punch Turn? Most people don't realize there are two completely different turns in hockey, and using the wrong one is what caps your speed without you ever knowing why.

The first is the tight turn. You reach for it when there's no room to cross but you still need to hold your speed through the corner. The second is the punch turn, and it does the opposite job. You use it when you want to scrub speed and cut even tighter to shake your opponent trying to check or strip the puck off of you.

Here's the part almost no one gets taught. These aren't the same skill at two settings. They're two separate tools for two different situations, and most players only ever own one of them with the punch never being used in games when it absolutely can be the difference between generating a scoring chance and not.

That's the gap between the NHL's best and everyone else. They have access to both and are so comfortable doing the mechanic from getting enough reps in training they show up automatically come game time when they need them most. 
Does the punch come automatic to you come game time? Forehand and backside side?

videos.title: Google AI Mode: The Future of Search… or Just Hype?
videos.summary_source_text:
Google AI Mode: The Future of Search… or Just Hype? Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Google quietly changed how Search works — and most people are still using it the old way. After weeks inside Google AI Mode, here's what's genuinely new, what's powering it, where it beats traditional search, and where it still falls apart.

Powered by Gemini 3 and the newer Gemini 3 Flash, AI Mode brings conversational follow-ups, multimodal input (voice, image, file), and Deep Search to a tool billions already use. But it's not all smooth — there are processing bugs, source-diversity questions, and a real publisher/privacy backlash (DuckDuckGo's "No AI" traffic tripled for a reason). This is the evidence-first breakdown of what's hype and what actually matters.
Drop a comment with where you land — love it, hate it, or still on the fence.

CHAPTERS
00:00 — What Google AI Mode Actually Is
1:08 — The Origin Story
2:42 — The Technology Behind It
4:19 — The Competitive Landscape
6:03 — The Real-World Experience
7:36 — The Verdict

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 vs Mythos 5: The Model Anthropic Locked Away
videos.summary_source_text:
Claude Fable 5 vs Mythos 5: The Model Anthropic Locked Away Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Anthropic just shipped two near-identically named frontier models — Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5 — and the marketing barely explains the difference. One you can use today. The other is gated behind Project Glasswing and reserved for vetted partners. Same underlying model, two deployment modes, very different rules.
In this video I cut through the launch-day noise: what Anthropic actually released, what the independent benchmarks say versus the press release, why there are two versions, what it costs, who can access it, how to actually get value out of it as a builder — and the honest answer to whether this is AGI. No hype, just the evidence.
Topics covered

What Fable 5 and Mythos 5 actually are (and why they're the same model)
CursorBench 3.1 results — 72.9% vs Opus 4.8, Opus 4.7, GPT-5.5
Databricks OfficeQA Pro — the speed and token-cost trade-off
Project Glasswing, the UK AISI cyber evaluation, and the bio-research angle
Pricing breakdown: $10 / $50 per million tokens vs Opus 4.8
How to actually deploy Fable 5 — effort levels, boundaries, verification
Is this AGI? The honest read

Chapters
00:00 The two-model problem nobody's explaining
01:06 What Anthropic actually launched
02:42 What it can do — and what the benchmarks actually show
04:56 The cost of all that autonomy
06:44 Why there are two versions — the safety story
08:32 Pricing and access
09:54 Getting the most out of it
10:45 So — is it AGI?
12:34 The takeaway

Watch next

CodeRabbit — Fable 5 code-review analysis

videos.summary_source_text:
I Let Claude Fable AI ACTUALLY Trade REAL Money For Me | INSANE Results 🚀 I Let Claude Fable AI ACTUALLY Trade REAL Money For Me | INSANE Results

Claude Fable 5 just dropped.

The benchmarks look insane.

But benchmarks don’t matter unless the model can actually do something useful.

So I decided to test it properly.

Not with theory.
Not with hype.
Not with fake screenshots.

I wanted to know three things:

1. Can Claude Fable 5 build a profitable TradingView strategy with no tools?
2. Can it use backtesting tools to improve and optimize that strategy?
3. Can it scan the market, take trades, monitor them, and manage the result?

So I gave Claude access to my AI trading workflow, my TradingView backtesting tools, my free Trader Dev MCP server, and my StrategyFactory skill.

Then I let it build strategies, backtest them, optimize them, and run a live AI trading test.

Some of it worked better than I expected.

Some of it didn’t.

But one thing genuinely surprised me…

Claude started monitoring its own trades and making decisions by itself.

👉 Access all of my trading bots: https://strategyfactory.ai/
🔥 FREE WORKBOOKS: https://app.strategyfactory.ai/resources/

---

## In this video you'll discover:

✅ What changed in the new Claude Fable 5 update
✅ Whether Claude can build a TradingView strategy with no tools
✅ Why AI trading needs real backtesting before you trust it
✅ How I connected Claude to TradingView using my free Trader Dev MCP server
✅ How Claude optimized strategies using automated backtests
✅ What happened when I let Claude scan the market and take live trades

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 & Mythos 5: So Powerful Anthropic Won't Let It Answer Everything
videos.summary_source_text:
Claude Fable 5 & Mythos 5: So Powerful Anthropic Won't Let It Answer Everything Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
You've sat through a dozen "most powerful model ever" launches by now — so when Anthropic shipped Claude Fable 5 and called it a Mythos-class model "made safe for the public," the easy reaction was: sure, marketing. Then the docs told a stranger story. This is a model so capable Anthropic had to build a system to stop it from answering certain questions — and that refusal is now a normal part of the product.

No hype, just the breakdown. We cover what Fable 5 and Mythos 5 actually are (and why one model got two names), how it holds up once you separate Anthropic's own demos from independent benchmarks, the new refusal-and-fallback behavior baked into the API, how to actually prompt it well, the price reality, the enterprise data-retention dealbreaker, and the question everyone asks in the comments: is this AGI?

CHAPTERS
00:00 The Strangest Part of This Launch
1:18 Fable vs. Mythos: The Same Brain, Two Doors
2:25 Why Anthropic Says This One Is Different
3:05 The Demos: Impressive, But They Picked Them
4:08 The Independent Numbers
5:22 The Catch: Refusal Is Now a Feature, Not a Bug
6:35 How to Actually Use It Well
8:00 Deployment, and the Enterprise Dealbreaker
8:55 The Price Tag
9:38 The Science Claims, Where It Gets a Little Unsettling
10:38 So... Is This AGI?
12:03 The Verdict

Which real workflow would YOU test Fable 5 on first — a big code migration, due diligence, or research synthesis? Drop it in the comments. The best answers usually shape the next video.

videos.title: LOW HANDS | Your Hands Are The Secret To A GREAT Golf Swing #shorts #golf #golfer #ericcogorno
videos.summary_source_text:
LOW HANDS | Your Hands Are The Secret To A GREAT Golf Swing #shorts #golf #golfer #ericcogorno #shorts 

In this video, Eric Cogorno demonstrates how to keep the hands low in the golf swing during the takeaway and downswing for incredible benefits! This simple technique helps you stay in posture in golf swing, keeps your left arm straight, and delivers better ball striking like PGA Tour Pros Nelly Korda and Adam Scott.

Keeping your hands low in the golf swing helps you stay in posture and keep your lead arm straighter. Watch how PGA Tour Pros Nelly Korda and Adam Scott execute perfect takeaway mechanics keeping their hands below the belt line and the club up. This method will help you to stop standing up in the golf swing and help you  play better golf with more consistent contact.

What You'll Learn:
• The "hands low, club up" mantra that transformed a student's ball striking
• Why keeping the hands low in golf swing helps golfers maintain proper golf posture
• How PGA Tour Pros Nelly Korda and Adam Scott position their hands during the takeaway and downswing
• How to keep the left shoulder down for consistent ball striking
• How to stay in posture in golf swing without thinking about it
• How to stop standing up in the golf swing by keeping the hands low in the golf swing
• Why low hands keep your lead arm straighter automatically
• Simple adjustments that help you how to play better golf

Most golfers don’t need more range time—they need a better plan. In this video, you’ll learn a simple 30-minute golf practice routine designed to improve your swing, ball striking, and consistency without spending hours at the course. This approach focuses on the fundamentals that actually lower scores: setup, takeaway, rotation, contact, and short game.

We’ll break down how to structure your practice session, which drills to prioritize, and how to avoid the common mistakes that lead to inconsistent shots. Whether you’re a beginner building fundamentals or an experienced golfer trying to sharpen your game, this routine helps you practice with purpose and see measurable improvement over time.

If you want straighter drives, cleaner iron shots, and a more repeatable golf swing, this 30-minute training plan is a great place to start.

🌟10 biological signals control how you feel every day, but only 1 matters most for YOUR body. Take the free 2-minute quiz to find out: https://drbrg.co/4dN4qoV

In this video, discover how one everyday beverage may destroy beneficial gut bacteria, damage your gut lining, and contribute to bloating, acid reflux, food sensitivities, and chronic digestive issues. The science behind this is shocking, and almost nobody is talking about it.

If your digestion feels off, your gut may be trying to warn you. Watch until the end to find the hidden culprit and the simple swap that can help support gut healing naturally.

Why You Keep Trying to Relax — But Your Body Won’t Let Go If you keep trying to relax your body but nothing seems to change, this video is for you.

Many people think tension means something is wrong and that they need to try harder to release it. But often, tightness is not a failure. It can be a learned protective response.

In this video, I share a gentler way to understand why your body may not be “letting go” — and how real ease begins through awareness, not force.

If this resonates with you and you want to go deeper into this work, you can learn more here: https://taroiwamoto.com/thecourse

videos.title: The Best and Worst Forms of Magnesium (Most People Get This Wrong)
videos.summary_source_text:
The Best and Worst Forms of Magnesium (Most People Get This Wrong) Not all magnesium is created equal, and taking the wrong type could be a complete waste of time and money.

🌟10 biological signals control how you feel every day, but only 1 matters most for YOUR body. Take the free 2-minute quiz to find out: https://drbrg.co/4vHOADq

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements.

In this video, we break down the best and worst forms of magnesium, including which ones are poorly absorbed and which ones your body can actually use.

Some forms of magnesium have extremely low absorption rates, meaning most of it passes right through your body. Others are far more bioavailable and can support sleep, relaxation, muscle recovery, energy, and even stress.

Watch until the end to discover which form stands out above the rest.

Dr. Eric Berg DC Bio:
Dr. Berg, age 61, is a chiropractor who specializes in Healthy Ketosis & Intermittent Fasting. He is the Director of Dr. Berg Nutritionals and author of the best-selling book The Healthy Keto Plan. He no longer practices, but focuses on health education through social media.

videos.title: After the Horn: Knicks Streak Snapped, Avs Coaching Questions & Broncos Offseason Buzz
videos.summary_source_text:
After the Horn: Knicks Streak Snapped, Avs Coaching Questions & Broncos Offseason Buzz The NBA Finals just got a lot more interesting.

On this episode of After the Horn, the crew breaks down the end of the New York Knicks' 13-game winning streak as Victor Wembanyama and the San Antonio Spurs fight back in the Finals. Was New York looking ahead? Has the momentum shifted for good?

Plus, the Colorado Avalanche offseason is officially underway. With Joe Sakic set to address the media, what does the future hold for head coach Jared Bednar? Should the Avs consider a coaching change, or is Bednar still the right man for the job?

The conversation also turns to the Stanley Cup Final, where Vegas and Carolina continue to deliver one of the most unpredictable championship series in recent memory.

The guys also discuss:
🏀 Knicks vs Spurs NBA Finals drama
🏒 Avalanche offseason questions and Jared Bednar's future
🏆 Stanley Cup Final reactions and predictions
🏈 Broncos minicamp updates, Jaylen Waddle hype & Jonathan Cooper news
🎓 The NCAA gambling controversy surrounding Brendan Sorsby
⚽ FIFA World Cup excitement and ticket controversy
⚾ Rockies trade deadline outlook and the future of the franchise

Join Ron Zappolo and Tom Green for another wide-ranging conversation covering the biggest stories in Colorado sports and beyond.

videos.title: Driver Made Simple — Put It All Together
videos.summary_source_text:
Driver Made Simple — Put It All Together Rotation ✅ Swing plane ✅ Weight shift ✅ Shoulder turn ✅ Face squaring ✅ Now it's time to add the ball. Run through each checkpoint — turn back, square the face, rotate through, hold your finish for 3 full seconds. Do that consistently and you'll be flushing drives you didn't think were possible. Save this post. This is the whole system.

The BEST driver under £150 😳⛳️ Welcome to H.I.T Golf Reviews – the home of honest, independent and trustworthy golf equipment reviews.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.

Our testing is built around launch monitor data (GCQuad) and real-world shots, but we always explain the numbers in normal-golfer language. No fluff, no hidden agendas – just whether a club will help your game or not.

Whether you’re:
buying your first proper set,
a mid-handicapper looking for more forgiveness, or
a low-handicap player chasing that perfect combo of feel and distance,

H.I.T is here to help you pick the right gear for your swing, not just the newest thing on the shelf.

Why H.I.T exists:
To cut through the hype.
To give golfers the truth.
To save you time, money and a few golf balls.
To make the game a bit easier – and more fun.

👋 WELCOME — I’M RICK SHIELS

This channel is all about helping you play better golf and enjoy the game more.
I make videos for every type of golfer — beginners, weekend players, improving golfers and low handicaps chasing new personal bests.

Here’s what you’ll find on the channel:

⭐ GOLF COACHING & LESSONS

I share simple, practical and proven golf instruction designed to help you:

• Fix your slice
• Fix your hook
• Hit your driver longer and straighter
• Improve your iron strike
• Add height, control and distance
• Learn how to draw and fade the golf ball
• Improve ball position, alignment and setup
• Build a repeatable, consistent golf swing
• Increase clubhead speed
• Improve swing path and club face control

You’ll also find full swing analysis, beginner advice, drills you can try at the range, and tips for getting better at golf quickly.

⭐ SHORT GAME & PUTTING

Lower your scores with short-game tips that help you:

• Stop duffing and thinning chips
• Improve contact and distance control
• Pitch closer to the hole
• Create more backspin
• Build confidence around the greens
• Read putts better
• Improve pace control and holing-out percentages

Short game is where most golfers drop shots — so I make sure you have everything you need to get better fast.

⭐ GOLF EQUIPMENT REVIEWS (H.I.T)

I test the latest:

• Drivers
• Irons
• Putters
• Wedges
• Golf balls
• Launch monitors
• Golf gadgets
• Budget vs premium clubs

All reviews are completely honest and independent. I use Trackman and GCQuad to give you accurate numbers you can trust.

If you’re trying to find the best golf driver, best irons, most forgiving clubs or value golf equipment — you’re in the right place.

⭐ BREAK 75 & GOLF ENTERTAINMENT

Break 75 has become one of YouTube’s biggest golf series.
You’ll see:

• Full 18-hole course vlogs
• Course management strategies
• Matches against tour players and creators
• Pressure moments, birdie runs and disaster holes
• Some of the world’s best golf courses
• Real, relatable golf

I also play matches and challenges with some of the biggest names in golf, including:

• Bryson DeChambeau
• Peter Finch
• Good Good
• GM Golf
• Bob Does Sports
• BustaJack
• Grant Horvat
• Me and My Golf
• Danny Maude

These help bring you the most entertaining and competitive golf content possible.

⭐ TRAVEL, COURSE VLOGS & LIV CONTENT

I film at some of the most incredible golf destinations around the world, including LIV tournament venues.
Expect:

• Amazing golf courses
• Behind-the-scenes access
• Player interactions
• Travel insights
• Bucket-list destinations
• Worldwide golf adventures

⭐ THE GOAL

To make golf more fun, more accessible and help you become a better, more confident, happier golfer — whether you’re breaking 100 or trying to break par. Be sure to subscribe to LIV Golf here:
https://www.youtube.com/@LIVGolf

If you’re trying to find the best golf driver, best irons, most forgiving clubs or value golf equipment — you’re in the right place.

⭐ BREAK 75 & GOLF ENTERTAINMENT

Break 75 has become one of YouTube’s biggest golf series.
You’ll see:

• Full 18-hole course vlogs
• Course management strategies
• Matches against tour players and creators
• Pressure moments, birdie runs and disaster holes
• Some of the world’s best golf courses
• Real, relatable golf

I also play matches and challenges with some of the biggest names in golf, including:

• Bryson DeChambeau
• Peter Finch
• Good Good
• GM Golf
• Bob Does Sports
• BustaJack
• Grant Horvat
• Me and My Golf
• Danny Maude

These help bring you the most entertaining and competitive golf content possible.

⭐ TRAVEL, COURSE VLOGS & LIV CONTENT

I film at some of the most incredible golf destinations around the world, including LIV tournament venues.
Expect:

• Amazing golf courses
• Behind-the-scenes access
• Player interactions
• Travel insights
• Bucket-list destinations
• Worldwide golf adventures

⭐ THE GOAL

To make golf more fun, more accessible and help you become a better, more confident, happier golfer — whether you’re breaking 100 or trying to break par.
